items.prompt: Which three sons of Noah are named in Genesis 10?
items.explanation: Genesis 10 begins with the family lines of Noah's sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

items.prompt: In what land did the people settle when they began building the city and tower?
items.explanation: Genesis 11 says the people settled on a plain in the land of Shinar.

items.prompt: Who went with Abram and Sarai when Abram left Haran in Genesis 12?
items.explanation: Genesis 12 says Lot went with Abram when Abram departed from Haran.

items.prompt: What happened to the people's language at Babel?

options: It was confused so they could not understand one another
It was written on stone tablets
It became the language of Egypt
It was spoken only by Abram's family

correct_answer.value: It was confused so they could not understand one another

items.explanation: Genesis 11 says the Lord confused their language so they could not understand one another's speech.

items.prompt: Why did Abram go down to Egypt in Genesis 12?

options: Because there was a famine in the land
Because Lot had already settled there

correct_answer.value: Because there was a famine in the land

items.explanation: Genesis 12 says Abram went down to Egypt because the famine in the land was severe.

items.prompt: Complete the sentence from Genesis 11.

content.text: The city was called ____ because there the Lord confused the language of all the earth.

items.explanation: Genesis 11 says the place was called Babel because the Lord confused the language there.

items.prompt: Complete the sentence from Genesis 12.

content.text: Abram set out to go to the land of ____.

items.explanation: Genesis 12 says Abram and those with him set out for the land of Canaan.

items.prompt: Complete the sentence from Genesis 12.

content.text: The Lord afflicted Pharaoh and his ____ because of Sarai.

items.explanation: Genesis 12 says the Lord afflicted Pharaoh and his house because of Sarai.

items.prompt: Complete the sentence from Genesis 11.

content.text: Terah took Abram, Lot, and Sarai and went out from Ur to go into the land of ____.

items.explanation: Genesis 11 says Terah set out from Ur intending to go to the land of Canaan.

items.prompt: Which image best represents the tower and city of Babel?

options.label: A rising tower above an ancient city on a plain
options.alt: Ancient city with a prominent tower rising above it

options.label: Small groups scattered across an open plain
options.alt: Groups of people moving away across a broad plain

options.label: A fortified city gate in an ancient settlement
options.alt: Ancient city gate with walls and road

options.label: A broad fertile plain with distant buildings
options.alt: Wide plain landscape with a distant settlement

correct_answer.value: A rising tower above an ancient city on a plain

items.explanation: Genesis 11 describes people building a city and a tower on the plain in Shinar.
items.image_accuracy_note: Images are symbolic teaching aids and are not exact historical reconstructions.

items.prompt: Which image best represents Abram traveling toward Canaan?

options.label: A caravan traveling toward distant hills
options.alt: Ancient caravan traveling toward the horizon

options.label: A desert caravan moving across dry country
options.alt: Travelers and pack animals crossing arid land

options.label: An Egyptian palace courtyard
options.alt: Ancient Egyptian palace or official house setting

options.label: A simple stone altar in open hill country
options.alt: Plain stone altar in an open landscape

correct_answer.value: A caravan traveling toward distant hills

items.explanation: Genesis 12 shows Abram departing as commanded and setting out toward Canaan.
items.image_accuracy_note: Images are symbolic teaching aids and are not exact historical reconstructions.

items.prompt: Put these Genesis 11 events in the order they appear.

items: The Lord scatters the people from there
The people begin building a city and tower

value: The people begin building a city and tower
The Lord scatters the people from there

items.explanation: Genesis 11 moves from settling in Shinar, to building, to language confusion, and then scattering.

items.prompt: Put these Genesis 12 events in the order they appear.

items: Abram builds an altar in Canaan

value: Abram builds an altar in Canaan

items.explanation: Genesis 12 moves from Abram's departure, to his altar in Canaan, to Egypt because of famine, and finally Pharaoh sending him away.

items.prompt: Put these Genesis 11–12 travel events in order.

content.instruction: Arrange these travel events in order.
items: Terah takes his family out from Ur
God tells Abram to go from his country
Abram leaves Haran with Sarai and Lot

value: Terah takes his family out from Ur
God tells Abram to go from his country
Abram leaves Haran with Sarai and Lot

items.explanation: Genesis 11 describes Terah leaving Ur and settling in Haran; Genesis 12 then describes God calling Abram and Abram leaving Haran.

items.prompt: Match each Genesis 10-12 name or place with the correct description.

content.instruction: Match each item with the correct description.
right_items: Plain where the people settled before building
King who later sent Abram away

value.right: Plain where the people settled before building
King who later sent Abram away

items.explanation: Genesis 11 and 12 directly connect these names and places to these events.

items.prompt: Match each Genesis 12 item with the correct description.

content.instruction: Match each item with the correct description.
right_items: Place where Abram went because of famine
Land Abram entered after leaving Haran
Built by Abram to the Lord

value.right: Land Abram entered after leaving Haran
Place where Abram went because of famine
Built by Abram to the Lord

items.explanation: Genesis 12 connects these people, places, and actions in Abram's travel account.

items.prompt: Match each Genesis 10–12 item with the correct passage detail.

content.instruction: Match each item with the correct passage detail.
right_items: Connected with being a mighty one on the earth
City name connected with confused language
Land/plain where the people settled before building
Ruler who sent Abram away from Egypt

value.right: Connected with being a mighty one on the earth
City name connected with confused language
Land/plain where the people settled before building
Ruler who sent Abram away from Egypt

items.explanation: These details are stated across Genesis 10, Genesis 11, and Genesis 12.
items.teaching_note: This item uses distinct passage facts instead of repeated matching descriptions.

items.prompt: Evaluate this statement from Genesis 11.

content.statement: Genesis 11 says the whole earth had one language and the same words.

items.explanation: Genesis 11 opens by saying the whole earth had one language and the same words.

items.prompt: Evaluate this statement from Genesis 12.

content.statement: Genesis 12 says Abram built altars in Canaan.

items.explanation: Genesis 12 records Abram building altars after entering the land.

items.prompt: Find the factual mistake in this Genesis 12 statement.

content.statement: After learning about Sarai, Pharaoh kept Abram in Egypt and refused to send him away.
content.instruction: Spot the error in this statement.
options: Pharaoh told Abram to leave and sent him away with what he had.
Pharaoh never heard anything about Sarai.
Abram had never gone down to Egypt.
Lot, not Pharaoh, took Sarai into his house.

correct_answer.value: Pharaoh told Abram to leave and sent him away with what he had.

items.explanation: Genesis 12 says Pharaoh commanded Abram to go and sent him away with his wife and all that he had.
items.teaching_note: This stays factual and non-sensational about the Egypt account.
